summary: forge init collision preflight reviewed over five Codex rounds (working-tree scope). Rounds 1-4 raised blocking findings - symlink escapes, blocking ancestor files, unchecked append/touch and mkdir-only destinations, gate-tree rogue files being blessed into the vendor manifest, then over-blocking of non-gate .claude/.codex content - each fixed and re-reviewed. Final round: no blocking defects; preflight covers scaffolded, generated, append-only, mkdir-only, and manifest-owned paths while preserving non-colliding files and --force behavior.

non_blocking_findings.summary: _would_write/APPEND_OR_TOUCH/ENSURED_DIRS must track cmd_init's copy loops; drift weakens the guard silently (docstring warns, gate tests pin WORKFLOW.md and .factory/reviews)
